Fix toolbar About button not appearing

commit.message:
fix: enforce tier rules on cached responses, add rules_version + role-keyed cache

1. Tier enforcement on all responses (cached or fresh):
   - enforceCitationTiers() demotes non-FP/non-reputable Tier A → B in citations
   - Hook evidence_tier fields also corrected before publishGateFinal
   - Stale cached payloads are re-cached with corrected tiers

2. rules_version in cache entries:
   - RULES_VERSION constant (bump on policy changes)
   - Stale version triggers re-caching after tier enforcement
   - DB migration: ALTER TABLE hook_cache ADD COLUMN rules_version

3. Cache key includes targetRole:
   - Each persona gets its own cache entry (URL::role=VP Sales)
   - Fixes cross-role cache pollution (CEO returning VP Sales hooks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add cargo check validation after metadata for cross-repo API detection

cargo metadata succeeds when crate versions exist on crates.io, but the
published API may differ from what the workspace code expects (e.g.
aprender uses entrenar modules not in the published version). Add a
cargo check --workspace --lib gate after metadata to catch this.

feat: conversational command center, pattern learning, TUI dashboard

Wire a full conversational loop: operator types intent → Pi agent responds
with farm intelligence → proposals appear inline → approvals/rejections
teach the system patterns → patterns gossip across the mesh.

- Add agent_response frame kind and UI subscriber tracking (chat.subscribe RPC)
- Redesign Command Center as chat-first UI with ChatMessage, ProposalCard,
  CitationBadge components
- Extract LLM responses from PiSession and broadcast to UI with conversation
  threading (conversationId/requestId)
- Add proposal approve/reject RPC handlers wired through to PiSession
- Implement PatternMemory module: learns from operator decisions, persists to
  ~/.clawmesh/mesh/patterns.json, gossips mature patterns across mesh
- Add TUI dashboard (mesh-tui.ts) with live peers, gossip, proposals, world
  model, and operator input
- Switch default model to google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview (API key via GEMINI_API_KEY)
- Fix UI WebSocket port to match node default (18789)

commit.message:
Fix pitch detection, MIDI output, and audio backend issues

- Fix WASAPI/ASIO sample rate mismatch: use actual device rate instead of
  hardcoding 44100 Hz (was causing all notes to detect ~1.5 semitones sharp)
- Add overlapping analysis windows (512-sample hop, 75% overlap) for ~4x
  faster pitch tracking updates
- Replace NAudio MidiOut with direct Win32 winmm.dll P/Invoke using
  CALLBACK_NULL to fix midiOutOpen failures on .NET 10 + Windows 11
- Make MIDI output failure non-fatal: warn and continue with pitch display
- Fix settings not applying live while running (sliders had no effect)
- Fix pitch bend jitter: use smoothed frequency instead of raw detection
- Fix dispose race condition: stop audio before disposing MIDI
- Fix note sustain: raise silence threshold for overlapping windows,
  lower min confidence to 0.5, track candidate note in hysteresis
- Add WASAPI (Exclusive) backend for low-latency capture without ASIO
- Add ASIO control panel button with proper sample rate negotiation
  (tries 48000, 44100, 96000, etc. instead of hardcoding 44100)
- Tune default smoothing (0.6) and note stability (3) for faster response

commit.message:
Add seoHead shortcode to centralize all meta tag generation

All SEO meta tags (title, description, robots, Open Graph, Twitter Card,
canonical) are now generated by a single JS shortcode in _11ty/shortcodes.js.
The layout calls {% seoHead title, description, tags, date %} instead of
27 lines of inline markup. Defaults, escaping, and blog-post detection
live in one place, so future pages are SEO-ready automatically.

commit.message:
Fix params=None causing SQLite error in _fetchone/_fetchall, breaking rpoll view registration

Default params from None to () so parameterless queries don't crash with
'parameters are of unsupported type'. This was the root cause of old polls
showing 'interaction failed' — _register_persistent_views() was silently
failing because get_all_active_rpolls() threw an exception.

commit.message:
fix(bootstrap): skip tailscale up if already connected, clearer error on failure

Previously tailscale up ran unconditionally if TS_AUTHKEY was set, which
logged out a working persisted session when the key expired. This is what
broke Tailscale after the recent deploy — the expired key in Coolify forced
a logout of a healthy node.

Now: check BackendState first. If Running, skip tailscale up entirely.
If not running and key is set, attempt auth with clear error on failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add diagnostic logging to logo upload and fetch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove redundant outer length check around truncate_str call (#182) (#192)

* fix: remove redundant outer length check around truncate_str call (#182)

Replace manual conditional truncation logic with existing cap_with_suffix helper,
which already handles both truncation and suffix appending. Makes cap_with_suffix
pub(crate) to expose this utility for use in agent.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move cap_with_suffix to text_util, its canonical home

Removes the cross-domain dependency from agent.rs → pr_review for a
generic string helper. cap_with_suffix now lives alongside truncate_str
in text_util; pr_review re-exports it via a use alias for internal callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: restrict text_util re-export visibility in pr_review

Change pub(crate) use to private use for cap_with_suffix and truncate_str.
These aliases are only used locally within pr_review.rs and were creating
misleading crate-wide namespace paths (e.g., crate::pr_review::truncate_str)
through a domain module. Restricting visibility prevents this namespace
pollution and clarifies that these are internal implementation details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: pre-size buffer in cap_with_suffix to avoid realloc on truncation path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: merge adjacent text_util imports into grouped use

Addresses dry-abstraction review suggestion — idiomatic Rust merges
same-module imports into a single `use` statement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(atoms): replace arrays instead of deep merging in mutate shorthand (#358)

Arrays in the `.mutate` shorthand now fully replace instead of deep
merging by index. The previous behavior was surprising - e.g.
`signal.mutate({ arr: [1, 2] })` on `[10, 20, 30]` would produce
`[1, 2, 30]`. Now it produces `[1, 2]`. Use the callback form for
index-level updates: `signal.mutate(s => { s.arr[2] = 30 })`.

Also fixes pre-existing type errors by loosening `AtomApiGenerics`
Signal constraint from `Signal` to `AnySignal`, and consolidates
`RecursivePartialWithArrayPlucking` into `RecursivePartial` (with an
array guard added to both core and atoms).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix gws auth login to stream stdout and open browser automatically

The login flow now:
1. Reads stdout asynchronously to detect the OAuth URL
2. Opens the URL in the user's default browser
3. Waits up to 2 min for the OAuth callback to complete

Previously the pipe was read synchronously after process exit, which
caused gws to fail because it blocks waiting for the browser redirect.

commit.message:
feat(ci): add release workflows for controller deb/container and agent deb/exe

Controller release (on v* tag):
- controller-deb: builds web UI, compiles controller, packages with nFPM
  into distributed-encoder-controller_<ver>_linux_amd64.deb
- controller-container: builds multi-stage Docker image and pushes to
  ghcr.io/<owner>/distributed-encoder-controller with semver tags

Agent release (on v* tag):
- agent-linux-deb: cross-compiles linux/amd64 binary, packages with new
  nfpm-agent.yaml into distributed-encoder-agent_<ver>_linux_amd64.deb
- agent-windows-exe: cross-compiles windows/amd64 binary, attaches
  distencoder-agent.exe to the GitHub Release

Supporting files added:
- nfpm-agent.yaml: nFPM descriptor for the Linux agent .deb
- configs/agent-linux.yaml.example: Linux-specific config template
- deployments/deb/distributed-encoder-agent.service: hardened systemd unit
- deployments/deb/agent-{postinstall,preremove,postremove}.sh: deb lifecycle scripts
  (creates distributed-encoder-agent system user, runtime dirs, env file;
  purge removes user and data but preserves /etc/distributed-encoder/ if shared)

ci.yml: add linux/amd64 agent build validation step alongside existing
windows/amd64 check so both targets are verified on every PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add per-game prediction system for real-time tournament updates (#76)

* Add tournament games: normalized game model, score recording, and game generation

Backend:
- Add core.tournament_games table with bracket_position, round, region tracking
- Add tournament format column to tournaments table
- Add TournamentGame model and SQLC queries (CRUD, list by tournament/round)
- Add TournamentGameRepository with full adapter implementation
- Add game generator that creates tournament games from bracket structure
- Extend bracket service with dual-write (select winner writes to both bracket
  and tournament_games), score recording, and game generation endpoints
- Add HTTP handlers: list games, get game, record score, generate games
- Add tournament game DTO with response mapping
- Add integration tests for repository and unit tests for generator/service/DTOs

Frontend:
- Add tournament games query keys (games, gameDetail)
- Add tournamentGame schema with Zod validation
- Add bracket service methods for game listing and score recording
- Enhance BracketGameCard and CompactGameCard with score display
- Add games section to TournamentViewPage
- Update useBracket hook with game-aware queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix generate-recaps build: pass gameRepo to bracket.New after signature change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extract TournamentGameRepository, auto-derive winner from scores, and display game status

- Extract TournamentGameRepository from TournamentRepository so game
  CRUD has its own dedicated struct and wiring
- Auto-derive winner in RecordScore when no winner is set, triggering
  the full SelectWinner flow (wins, elimination, propagation)
- Use COALESCE in UpdateTournamentGameResult to preserve existing scores
  when NULL is passed (fixes score wipe on dual-write)
- Add game status field to BracketGame model, DTO, and frontend schema
- Display "Final" and "Live" status badges in bracket UI cards
- Enqueue prediction refresh in recordScoreHandler when winner is derived

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add backfill-games CLI tool to materialize historical tournament games

Adds a CLI tool that reconstructs tournament_games rows for historical
tournaments (2016-2025) by reusing the bracket builder and result
application logic. Scores are placeholder (1-0) since historical scores
were never captured. Also fixes tournamentGameToCreateParams to pass
through scores instead of hardcoding nil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use tournament games as source of truth for bracket rendering

Replace the dual applyCurrentResults + score overlay with a single
applyTournamentGames pure function that reads winners, scores, status,
and team assignments directly from tournament game records. Falls back
to the old win/bye-counter approach when no games exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ESPN score import service, school aliases, and score-authoritative winner derivation

Introduces a score import pipeline that fetches real game scores and times
from ESPN's scoreboard API and records them against tournament games. Key
changes:

- ESPN adapter: HTTP client, response parsing, school name alias mapping
- Score import service: matches ESPN games to tournament games by team pairs,
  records scores chronologically, rebuilds game index after each recording
  to handle winner corrections that propagate to later-round slots
- RecordScore now derives winners from scores and corrects mismatches
  instead of rejecting them, making the import authoritative over
  hand-entered data
- Score import worker for polling during live tournaments
- Backfill-games safety: refuse to regenerate if games have recorded results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove obsolete test for inconsistent winner/score rejection

RecordScore now auto-derives the winner from scores rather than
rejecting inconsistent combinations, making this test invalid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add per-game prediction system for real-time tournament updates

Instead of regenerating predictions only when an entire round completes,
the simulator now locks in decided games immediately. After each game goes
final, predictions regenerate with that outcome locked in and remaining
games simulated — enabling real-time prediction updates during the tournament.

Key changes:
- New decided_game_count column on prediction_batches (migration)
- Game-aware matchup generator that deterministically resolves decided games
- BuildDecidedByTeamPair / ComputeThroughRoundFromGames helpers
- RunGameAware service method used by the core compute worker
- Standings dedup switched from through_round to decided_game_count

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add bracket game scores and improved tournament view UI

Includes score display on bracket games, updated frontend bracket
rendering, and DTO changes to support slot scores.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TypeScript build errors in bracket test helpers

Add required `status` default to makeGame helpers — Zod's
`.optional().default()` produces a non-optional output type, so
manually constructed BracketGame objects need the field set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add completed_at to tournament games for prediction timeline

Records when a game result was finalized. Set automatically by
UpdateGameResult when status transitions to 'final' (uses NOW() on
first write, preserves existing value on re-record). Cleared on
ClearGameResult. Enables using real timestamps on the prediction
bump chart x-axis instead of just decided_game_count.

For historical games, completed_at can be backfilled by estimating
from scheduled_at + average game duration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
